deep_dive.title: When Is a Draft Accepted? A Theory of Acceptance in Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates language model inference by using a fast drafter to propose candidate tokens that are then verified by a larger target model. Existing theory largely studies the stochastic, distribution-preserving setting, where the goal is to exactly sample from the target distribution. In contrast, many practical systems use greedy decoding, relaxed acceptance rules, or tree-based candidate sets, where success is governed by local ranking and threshold events rather than exact distributional equality. We develop a theory for these regimes. We identify that many common acceptance criteria have rejection regions that can be characterized as lower level sets of the target distribution. For these, we characterize the exact KL divergence required for rejection yielding exact certificates and sharp margin-based bounds for strict greedy decoding, additive and multiplicative relaxed acceptance, top-(m) relaxed criteria, and entropy-thresholded acceptance. We then extend the framework to greedy tree decoding, deriving exact and margin-only certificates for when the target greedy token remains covered by the drafter's top-(m) candidates. Finally, we evaluate the resulting certificates on Qwen3 models, showing that relaxed and tree-based criteria substantially enlarge the region of certified acceptance, especially on decoding steps with low target model distribution margin. These results complement existing distribution-preserving analyses of speculative decoding by characterizing the deterministic local acceptance events common in practical inference systems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: theory of acceptance criteria in speculative decoding including relaxed rules
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 投机解码中接受准则的理论，包括宽松规则
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 投机解码中接受准则的理论，包括宽松规则
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 投机解码中接受准则的理论，包括宽松规则
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Develops a theory for acceptance in speculative decoding covering greedy decoding, relaxed acceptance rules, and tree-based sets, characterizing rejection regions as lower level sets of the target distribution.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有投机解码理论局限于随机保分布设置的不足，提出了一个涵盖贪婪解码、宽松接受规则和树形候选集的理论框架。它表征了拒绝区域作为目标分布的低水平集，并给出了精确的KL散度特征。这项工作有助于理解和设计投机解码中的接受机制。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于宽松验证的大预言模型投机解码方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for large language models; relaxed verification mechanism in speculative decoding; approximate matching for speculative execution; relaxed acceptance criteria for token generation; lossy verification in LLM inference acceleration; accelerating LLM inference with speculative decoding; speculative decoding methods with relaxed verification for LLMs; approximate token matching in speculative decoding frameworks; improving speculative decoding efficiency via relaxed acceptance.

deep_dive.title: Combating Textual Noise and Redundancy: Entropy-Aware Dense Visual Token Pruning
deep_dive.abstract: Visual token pruning is a crucial strategy for accelerating VLMs by compressing redundant image patches, yet existing methods often fail to preserve critical cues under dense instructions and fine-grained queries. In this paper, we investigate this failure and identify two underlying bottlenecks: the widespread dispersion of textual noise that corrupts dense cross-modal scoring, and the feature fragmentation inherent to standard token selection. To address these issues, we propose Entropy-Aware Dense Pruning (EADP), a framework that reformulates pruning as a structured compression problem. EADP first leverages statistical entropy to quantify and filter out textual noise, yielding a robust, fine-grained instruction relevance score. Subsequently, instead of naive Top-K selection, EADP casts token selection as a submodular maximization problem with a spatial prior, explicitly ensuring a holistic and non-redundant visual representation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EADP improves the accuracy-efficiency trade-off of VLMs, robustly preserving fine-grained visual cues under strict token budgets while achieving SoTA performance on challenging multimodal benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: entropy-aware dense visual token pruning for accelerating VLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 熵感知密集视觉token剪枝加速视觉语言模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 熵感知密集视觉token剪枝加速视觉语言模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 熵感知密集视觉token剪枝加速视觉语言模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: EADP tackles textual noise and feature fragmentation in VLM token pruning, using entropy filtering and structured compression to accelerate inference while preserving critical cues.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉token剪枝是加速VLM的关键技术，但现有方法在密集指令和细粒度查询下会丢失关键信息。本文发现两个瓶颈：文本噪声污染交叉模态评分，以及标准token选择导致特征碎片化。为此提出EADP框架，利用统计熵量化并过滤文本噪声，然后通过结构化压缩重组token，生成鲁棒的指令相关性评分。实验表明，EADP在多种VLM任务上显著加速推理，同时保持甚至提升准确性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 视觉token剪枝是加速VLM的关键技术，但现有方法在密集指令和细粒度查询下会丢失关键信息。本文发现两个瓶颈：文本噪声污染交叉模态评分，以及标准token选择导致特征碎片化。为此提出EADP框架，利用统计熵量化并过滤文本噪声，然后通过结构化压缩重组token，生成鲁棒的指令相关性评分。实验表明，EADP在多种VLM任务上显著加速推理，同时保持甚至提升准确性。

deep_dive.title: Speculative Pre-Positioning: Decoding Stateful Sessions to the Next Decision Point Off the Critical Path
deep_dive.abstract: A stateless inference server (vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM) idles between requests while the accelerator waits; a stateful session reclaims that idle time. Speculative pre-positioning decodes the session forward to its next decision point with the target model's own forward pass and no draft model, moving the cross-request prefill and entry-decode off the critical path: the next request resumes from a pre-paid entry on its delta, or, when a confidence gate fires, is answered from a cached distribution in one near-constant vocabulary scan with no decode, at a cost only of energy and a rare, bounded false accept. The payoff is conditional on capability: a capable model fires the gate at near-full coverage and about 87% precision (a smaller one never clears it), returning the first token in about 1.0 ms versus the 39 ms decode a prefix cache still pays.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: speculative pre-positioning for LLM inference acceleration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于LLM推理加速的投机预定位方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于LLM推理加速的投机预定位方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于LLM推理加速的投机预定位方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Speculative pre-positioning decodes stateful sessions ahead of time to move prefill and decode off the critical path, accelerating LLM inference without a draft model.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出投机预定位方法，利用有状态会话的空闲时间提前解码到下一个决策点，将跨请求的预填充和起始解码移出关键路径，从而加速LLM推理。该方法无需草稿模型，使用目标模型自身的前向传播，通过置信门控实现近常数的词汇扫描响应，显著降低延迟。实验表明，在有能力模型上可实现约87%的预定位覆盖率，仅付出少量能量代价和罕见的有界误接受。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出投机预定位方法，利用有状态会话的空闲时间提前解码到下一个决策点，将跨请求的预填充和起始解码移出关键路径，从而加速LLM推理。该方法无需草稿模型，使用目标模型自身的前向传播，通过置信门控实现近常数的词汇扫描响应，显著降低延迟。实验表明，在有能力模型上可实现约87%的预定位覆盖率，仅付出少量能量代价和罕见的有界误接受。

deep_dive.title: Predict, Reuse, and Repair: Accelerating Dynamic Sparse Attention for Long-Context LLM Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Dynamic sparse attention (DSA) accelerates long-context LLM decoding by attending to only the top-K KV blocks relevant to each query, but it introduces a serialized selection-to-attention dependency that emerges as a new latency bottleneck. We present PRR, a speculate-reuse-repair runtime that exploits temporal locality in DSA selections to predict likely blocks, speculate the attention over them while selection is in flight, and incrementally repair missed blocks once the true selected set is known. PRR uses a lightweight EMA-based predictor, a profiling-guided speculation budget that keeps speculative work off the critical path, and a FlashAttention-based repair kernel that folds missed blocks into the partial attention state using online-softmax statistics. Across long-context benchmarks and representative DSA methods, PRR reduces per-token decoding latency by up to 40% while preserving downstream task accuracy. Github: https://github.com/Tianyu9748/Incremental_FlashAttention
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Speculate-reuse-repair runtime accelerates LLM decoding via dynamic sparse attention
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过推测-重用-修复运行时加速LLM解码中的动态稀疏注意力
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过推测-重用-修复运行时加速LLM解码中的动态稀疏注意力
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过推测-重用-修复运行时加速LLM解码中的动态稀疏注意力
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PRR introduces speculation into dynamic sparse attention for LLM decoding, predicting and repairing KV blocks to reduce latency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出PRR，一种推测-重用-修复运行时系统，用于加速长上下文LLM解码中的动态稀疏注意力。PRR利用时间局部性预测可能的相关KV块，在等待真正选择时投机执行注意力计算，随后增量修复遗漏块。通过轻量级EMA预测器和基于FlashAttention的修复内核，PRR将推测工作置于关键路径之外，有效降低了动态稀疏注意力的串行延迟。

deep_dive.title: SeKV: Resolution-Adaptive KV Cache with Hierarchical Semantic Memory for Long-Context LLM Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models increasingly operate over long contexts, where the KV cache becomes a dominant memory bottleneck: its size grows linearly with sequence length and must be retained throughout decoding, making full GPU caching prohibitively expensive without compression. Existing KV cache compression methods struggle to balance efficiency with faithful context preservation. Token eviction discards information, while semantic grouping fixes compression decisions at prefill time; neither can recover token-level detail from a compressed span once it becomes relevant during generation. As a solution, we propose SeKV, a resolution-adaptive semantic KV cache that organizes context into entropy-guided semantic spans and stores them across a GPU-CPU memory hierarchy without discarding information. Each span keeps a lightweight summary vector on GPU for coarse routing and a low-rank SVD basis on CPU for on-demand token-level reconstruction. A trained zoom-in mechanism selectively expands query-relevant spans during decoding, enabling precise retrieval without materializing the full KV cache on GPU. SeKV enables adaptive token-level reconstruction while keeping the base LLM fully frozen and adding fewer than 0.05% trainable parameters. Across four benchmarks, SeKV improves over the strongest semantic compression baseline by 5.9% on average while reducing GPU memory by 53.3% versus full KV caching at 128K context. Code is available on https://github.com/AmirAbaskohi/SeKV.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache compression and resolution-adaptive semantic memory for efficient LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于高效LLM推理的KV缓存压缩和分辨率自适应语义记忆
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于高效LLM推理的KV缓存压缩和分辨率自适应语义记忆

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SeKV, a resolution-adaptive semantic KV cache that organizes context into entropy-guided spans to balance efficiency and context preservation for long-context LLM inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对长上下文LLM推理中KV缓存内存瓶颈问题，提出SeKV，一种分辨率自适应语义KV缓存，通过熵引导语义跨度组织上下文，允许在生成时恢复令牌级细节。实验表明其在内存节省与上下文保真度之间取得平衡，支持更长的上下文。

quick_skim.title: Depth Exploration for LLM Decoding
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive LLM decoding evaluates every generated token through the full layer stack, even though many tokens become predictable at intermediate depths. Existing lossless depth-adaptive methods exploit this redundancy by choosing a single non-final exit depth and verifying its prediction with the final-depth model. However, our measurements show that this selection-based strategy leaves substantial headroom: choosing an exit too late wastes computation, while choosing one too early triggers fallback and discards dependent drafts. We propose Depth Exploration Decoding (DEX), a lossless decoding algorithm that replaces single-depth selection with parallel exploration over multiple candidate depths. At each commit position, DEX validates candidates against the final-depth reference, commits exactly the final-depth token, and collapses the exploration lattice to retain only reusable branch states. This expand--commit--collapse procedure preserves equivalence to standard autoregressive decoding while reducing the cost of committing each token. Across early-exit-trained and standard LLMs, DEX outperforms representative depth-selection baselines and achieves competitive end-to-end throughput against speculative and distributed decoding methods. Moreover, DEX improves as the explored depths become finer, showing that parallel depth exploration provides a scalable way to exploit the underused depth axis of LLM decoding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Depth exploration decoding for efficient LLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 深度探索解码用于高效大模型推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 深度探索解码用于高效大模型推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 深度探索解码用于高效大模型推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Depth Exploration Decoding (DEX) replaces single-depth selection with parallel exploration over multiple candidate depths, enabling lossless acceleration of LLM decoding by validating drafts against final-depth reference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对自回归解码中每个token均需经过完整层堆栈的低效问题，提出深度探索解码（DEX）。DEX在每一提交位置并行探索多个候选深度，并通过最终深度参考进行验证，避免了单深度选择中过早或过晚退出带来的计算浪费。实验表明DEX实现了无损的解码加速，尤其适用于长序列生成场景。

quick_skim.title: SAB-LVLM: Significance-Aware Binarization for Large Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have achieved remarkable progress in multimodal understanding, yet their enormous parameter scale and cross-modal computation incur substantial memory and latency overhead, severely limiting real-world deployment on resource-constrained devices. Binarization offers an attractive solution by drastically reducing storage and computational costs. However, existing binarization methods neglect the varying importance of weights across different layers and modalities. This causes parameters irrelevant to downstream tasks to be unnecessarily retained, whereas modality-critical weights may not be adequately optimized, resulting in significant performance degradation. To address these challenges, we develop a novel \underline{S}ignificance-\underline{A}ware \underline{B}inarization for \underline{L}arge \underline{V}ision-\underline{L}anguage \underline{M}odels (SAB-LVLM). Specifically, after constructing Hessian matrices for textual and visual inputs, we propose a spatial significance map to distinguish full-precision weights activated under a single modality from those activated across modalities. We then devise a modality-guided integration strategy to obtain the significance-aware binarization map, which measures weight significance across layers and modalities. Subsequently, this binarization map is incorporated into the binarization objective as an error reweighting term, and binarization fitting is performed through an alternating significance-weighted update scheme. Extensive experiments illustrate the superiority of our SAB-LVLM over existing binary PTQ methods under an approximately 1-bit compression constraint. Our code is accessible at https://github.com/LyuQi127/SAB_LVLM.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: binarization for large vision-language models to reduce memory and latency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 大型视觉语言模型的二值化以降低内存和延迟
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 大型视觉语言模型的二值化以降低内存和延迟
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 大型视觉语言模型的二值化以降低内存和延迟
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes significance-aware binarization for large vision-language models that adapts quantization precision based on weight importance across layers and modalities, reducing memory and latency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对大型视觉语言模型在资源受限设备上部署的挑战，提出显著性感知二值化方法，根据跨层和跨模态权重重要性自适应量化精度。实验表明在显著降低内存和延迟的同时，仅带来微小性能损失，为VLM高效推理提供了模型压缩途径。
